title: Fluorescence microscopy of Chlamydomonas reinhardtii - mCherry | Chlorophyll
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a6v8i","text":"This protocol describe the steps to perform Chlamydomonas reinhardtii fluorescence microscopy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":44,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3cdjv","text":"Cells should be cultured to a late log phase to increase cell number for microscopy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Grow cells to late-log phase density (~1 × 10 7 cells/mL) in Tris-acetate-phosphate (TAP) medium at 25°C under constant illumination of 50 μmol photons/m 2 s at 150 rpm on a rotary shaker

Prepare a 1% agarose TAP media, by adding sufficient agarose to TAP media and dissolve agarose by heating in a microwave. Pour the melted media in a agar pad slide, or prepare a glass slide with a double adjacent slides barrier, forming a wall around the pad slide. Add slides on top of the poured media to allow formation of a smooth surface Remove adjacent and top slides, and add 10 µL of culture to a desired spot on the solid media Add a coverslip above the sample a
Place slide on microscope Use a argon laser at 543 nm to excite mCherry and a spectral detector set at 610-650 nm. Record images.
Place slide on microscope Use a laser at 405 nm to excite chlorophyll and a spectral detector set at 680 nm. Record images.
For stack images use 0.4 µm steps per picture in z-axis
creator: Joao Vitor Molino
